code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test25-MAR-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria and glucosuria, with low specific gravity, suggesting renal tubular dysfunction (possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy). No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. The findings warrant further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying diabetes or other renal pathology.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
